shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,L,W,L. value_profile_cmplog RESOLVES; value_profile AND naive BLOCK; cmplog NON-decisive. The winner is the only decisive arm carrying I2SRandReplace (the resolve is attributed to the I2S inside vpc), so this is an i2s-pro shape whose winner is vpc rather than plain cmplog.
round_note: FINAL (round-3) re-design. 78 confirmed FROZEN + 12 unlabeled (10 rule_not_met, 2 seed_starved). Priors operand_enrichment (i2s_exact_literal_gate) AND value_distance_reached (i2s_multibyte_operand_gradient_stall) are both KEEP/decidable:true and carried VERBATIM (superset/monotonic). The 5 named members of the value_distance_reached hypothesis (harfbuzz_5745/5362, curl_427, libxml2_6233/7249) came back rule_not_met for THEM specifically (the wide-FOURCC corpora let a sliding Hamming window find coincidental near-matches in loser byte-soup, so distance_gap washed out -- exactly the registry's short/dispersed-operand caveat), but the rule confirmed >=1 branch elsewhere in the shape, so it is retained unchanged. This round proposes ONE genuinely-new decidable mechanism via the UNTRIED joint_necessity tool for the structured-font sub-group, and routes everything else to honest decidable:false (with the two seed_starved branches flagged for rank-10 re-bisect).

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves an exact-literal equality gate because its I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand literal into a fixed input offset, which value_profile's Hamming/CMP_MAP gradient cannot land

params.arm_repointing: THIS SHAPE'S WINNER IS vpc, NOT cmplog. operand_enrichment as built computes cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment. Repoint it to measure the SAME byte_freq_ratio on the vpc-RESOLVING corpus (the I2S-bearing winner) vs the union of the value_profile- and naive-BLOCKING corpora (the non-I2S losers). signed_target_enrich = log2( freq(target literal byte @ gate offset in vpc corpus) / freq(same byte in vp+naive corpora) ). This is a PARAM change (which corpus dirs feed W vs L), not a new tool: the measurement, metrics, and gate-offset extraction are identical.
params.gate_offset_source: per-branch W/L decisive seed bytes already in db/blockers.sqlite (resolving_seeds vs blocking_seeds); the gate offset + target literal come from the branch's operand_literal / byte diff, exactly as the validated harfbuzz/5391 anchor.
params.scope: scored ONLY for targets with on-disk corpora and a FIXED-OFFSET contiguous literal: harfbuzz + curl here. lcms/libxml2/bloaty/libpng lack on-disk corpus (data_reality) and fall to fallback:inconclusive for the evidence-confirmation guardrail, though they still carry the shape's Layer-1 label.

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich > 0 (strictly): the exact target literal at the gate offset is MORE frequent in the vpc-resolving corpus than in the vp/naive-blocking corpora, because only the I2S-bearing winner can place the all-or-nothing constant. An ALTERNATIVE mechanism predicts differently: if vpc resolved via value_profile's gradient alone (not I2S substitution), the literal would accrete byte-by-byte in the value_profile corpus too and signed_target_enrich would be ~0 (no winner-over-loser enrichment, since vp is a loser arm). If vpc resolved by some structural/length route rather than landing THIS literal, the tool returns no_gate_signature. So a positive sign is a discriminating confirmation of I2S literal substitution, not merely of 'vpc wins'.
hypotheses.rule_notes: Threshold 1.0 (>=2x relative frequency, log2) is conservative vs the validated pro median of +3.12 at the operand_enrichment anchor; > 0 alone is the falsifiable boundary, 1.0 guards against corpus-frequency noise. FROZEN: 78 branches confirmed under this rule in the prior rounds; carried forward unchanged so they re-validate (superset/monotonic).

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a multi-byte exact-equality token gate by landing the EXACT logged comparand (vpc reaches Hamming distance ~0 to the operand), while value_profile -- despite carrying the SAME CMP_MAP Hamming feedback as vpc -- STALLS at a non-zero distance because the wide all-or-nothing equality offers no climbable gradient; only I2SRandReplace's one-shot operand substitution crosses the final gap
hypotheses.round3_status: KEEP / FROZEN (superset). Confirmed >=1 branch in prior rounds, so retained VERBATIM. Its 5 listed members came back rule_not_met for them specifically (wide/dispersed-FOURCC corpora let the sliding Hamming window find coincidental near-matches in loser byte-soup -> distance_gap washed out). Those members are re-routed below: harfbuzz_5745+5362 to the NEW joint_necessity structural-assembly test; curl_427 + libxml2_6233/7249 to decidable:false (no remaining built tool discriminates their wide-token assembly).

params.arm_repointing: value_distance_reached's anchor (sqlite3/14437) already has the vpc=winner pattern: it reads, per arm, the MIN over the arm's seeds of (min over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width). Repoint W = vpc-resolving seeds, L = union of value_profile- and naive-BLOCKING seeds. The discriminating contrast here is vpc_min_distance (winner, has I2S) vs vp_min_distance (loser, has the SAME CMP_MAP Hamming feedback but NO I2SRandReplace). distance_gap = vp_min_distance - vpc_min_distance; winner_closer = (vpc_min_distance < vp_min_distance). The Hamming-window computation is identical; only the arm-to-role mapping is set per this shape.

operand_per_branch.harfbuzz_5745: 'cmap' (63 6d 61 70) at header offset ~0x0c and/or sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 at 0x00 (width 4)
operand_per_branch.harfbuzz_5362: 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) at table-tag offset ~0x0c and/or sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 (width 4)
operand_per_branch.libxml2_6233: 'xml:id' (78 6d 6c 3a 69 64) attribute-fullname literal (width 6) -- supplied by the owning server sB
operand_per_branch.libxml2_7249: 'xml:id' / 'id="' attribute literal (width 4-6) -- supplied by the owning server sB

params.scope: On s4 (on-disk corpora) value_distance_reached scores harfbuzz_5745, harfbuzz_5362, curl_427. libxml2_6233 + libxml2_7249 are sB targets: VALID test, arbitrated by the owning server with the same tool + the 'xml:id' operand (cross-server rule -- do NOT mark decidable:false for corpus-absence on the running server). All five operands are width>=4, clear of the registry's width<=2 chance-alignment caveat.

hypotheses.prediction: winner_closer == true AND vpc_min_distance ~ 0 AND vp_min_distance strictly > 0 (distance_gap large): the vpc-resolving corpus reaches the EXACT multi-byte operand (distance ~0) while the value_profile-blocking corpus -- which has identical CMP_MAP Hamming feedback minus I2SRandReplace -- stalls at a non-zero minimum distance, the all-or-nothing equality giving its gradient nowhere to climb. DISCRIMINATING vs the obvious alternatives: (1) if value_profile's Hamming gradient could itself reach the operand, vp_min_distance would also fall to ~0 and distance_gap ~ 0 -> the I2S-exact-substitution claim is REFUTED (vp would resolve too). (2) If vpc resolved by assembling a larger STRUCTURE rather than landing THIS operand (the token-assembly story), vpc_min_distance to the specific operand would NOT be ~0 and winner_closer would not hold -> picks against the assembly mechanism. So vpc-at-zero-while-vp-stalls is a confirmation of the exact multi-byte I2S substitution specifically, independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape (G2): both arms carry the same CMP_MAP feedback, so the distance gap is attributable to I2SRandReplace, not to 'vpc is the winner'.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND vpc_min_distance <= 0.2 AND distance_gap >= 0.25
hypotheses.rule_notes: vpc_min_distance <= 0.2 = winner lands at or essentially at the exact operand (<=20% of operand bytes off, i.e. an exact or near-exact splice). distance_gap >= 0.25 = the value_profile gradient stalls at least a quarter of the operand width short of vpc, the falsifiable separation. winner_closer must also be true so the gap is in the correct direction. Conservative vs the anchor (vpc dist 0 vs cmp/naive 0.5, gap 0.5); 0.25 guards short-operand noise. ALL three conjuncts (AND) so the confirmation is of I2S exact-operand placement, not a marginal closeness difference. FROZEN this round.

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a font-structure presence gate (glyph/table lookup succeeds) by assembling a LARGER, fuller sfnt table directory than the value_profile/naive corpora ever build -- the vpc-resolving seeds carry many valid 4-byte sfnt table tags (a structured directory), while the blocking corpora are unstructured byte-soup with ~0 valid tags; the resolve is driven by structural depth (more assembled tags), not by landing one specific comparand
hypotheses.novelty_note: Genuinely different mechanism AND tool from BOTH prior decidable tests already refuted on these two branches. (1) operand_enrichment (i2s_exact_literal_gate, round-1) measured byte FREQUENCY of one fixed-offset literal byte -- it did not hold: the winner seeds carry the literal embedded in a DISPERSED structured directory (e.g. harfbuzz_5745 'cmap' at ~0x0c plus 'GSUN' near 0x1b plus sfnt magic at 0x00), so a single fixed-offset frequency ratio washes out. (2) value_distance_reached (i2s_multibyte_operand_gradient_stall) measured Hamming DISTANCE to ONE operand -- it also did not hold here: with a single 4-byte FOURCC over a wide byte-soup loser corpus, a sliding window finds coincidental near-matches, so distance_gap collapses (the registry's dispersed-operand caveat). The NEW observable is the joint_necessity STRUCTURAL signal: median seed SIZE + the COUNT of valid sfnt table tags carried by the vpc-resolving vs vp/naive-blocking seed sets. The card evidence is explicit: harfbuzz_5745/5362 winners are 'a consistent structured table directory' / 'a valid sfnt font' while losers are 'unstructured byte soup' / 'mangled/short blobs'. This is joint_necessity's ASSEMBLY-DEPTH subtype, the UNTRIED tool for this shape (registry anchor harfbuzz/6814, validated on font tags), and harfbuzz is exactly its validated --tokens vocabulary (sfnt 4-byte tags). It is NOT a relaxed re-run of either refuted rule -- different metric (structural tag count + size lift), different tool.

params.arm_repointing: joint_necessity as built reports per-set median size + structural-tag count for the vpc-RESOLVING vs cmplog-BLOCKING vs value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets. THIS shape's losers are value_profile + naive (cmplog is non-decisive _). Repoint: vpc_tags = tag count of the vpc-resolving set; vp_tags = tag count of the value_profile-blocking set (the discriminating loser, since vp carries the SAME CMP_MAP feedback as vpc, so a tag-count gap isolates the I2S MUTATOR's structure-building, not the feedback). tag_lift = vpc_tags - vp_tags (or vpc/vp ratio per the tool's convention); size_lift = median(vpc seed size) / median(vp seed size). The size/token computation is identical to the anchor; only the loser arm is set to value_profile (and naive as the secondary loser).
params.tokens: harfbuzz sfnt 4-byte table tags: cmap,GPOS,GSUB,glyf,head,hhea,hmtx,maxp,name,post,OS/2,loca,morx,kern,COLR,CPAL,feat,trak (the validated harfbuzz --tokens set used at the joint_necessity anchor). PLUS the sfnt version magic 00 01 00 00 / OTTO as a directory-presence marker.
params.scope: harfbuzz on-disk corpus exists on s4 -> both members (harfbuzz_5745, harfbuzz_5362) are scored on s4. No cross-server dependency (both are harfbuzz). Needs >=3 vpc-resolving and >=3 value_profile-blocking seeds in db/blockers.sqlite -- the round-2 assignments report both as seed-present (no longer seed-starved).

hypotheses.prediction: vpc_tags significantly > vp_tags AND size_lift >= 1.3 (the vpc-resolving corpus carries a fuller, larger assembled sfnt directory than the value_profile-blocking corpus). DISCRIMINATING vs the obvious alternatives: (1) if vpc resolved by landing ONE specific operand rather than assembling depth, vpc_tags would NOT exceed vp_tags by much (one tag, not a directory) and the prior value_distance_reached test would already have caught it -- it did not, so a tag-COUNT lift here is the alternative signal, picking structural assembly over single-operand substitution. (2) if value_profile's gradient could itself build the structure, vp_tags would approach vpc_tags and tag_lift ~ 0 -> the assembly-depth claim REFUTES (vp would resolve too, but vp is a blocking arm). (3) if the resolve were driven purely by seed SIZE with no real structure, vpc_tags would be ~0 despite size_lift>1 -> the tag-count conjunct fails and we do NOT over-claim. So vpc-builds-more-valid-tags-AND-bigger, while vp (same feedback, no I2S mutator) stays tag-poor, is a discriminating confirmation of I2S-driven structural assembly. G2 independence: value_profile and vpc share the CMP_MAP feedback, so the tag/size gap is attributable to the I2SRandReplace mutator building structure, orthogonal to 'vpc is the winner'.
hypotheses.rule: vpc_tags > vp_tags AND tag_lift >= 2.0 AND size_lift >= 1.3
hypotheses.rule_notes: tag_lift >= 2.0 = the vpc-resolving set carries at least ~2x the valid sfnt tags of the value_profile-blocking set (a built directory vs byte-soup), conservative vs the assembly-depth anchor where joint_confirmed sets show a clear tag gap. size_lift >= 1.3 = the resolving seeds are at least 30% larger (a real table directory occupies more bytes than a soup blob). vpc_tags > vp_tags is the directional guard. ALL three conjuncts (AND) so the confirmation is of genuine structural assembly (more valid tags AND bigger), not a size artifact and not a single-operand placement (which the prior value_distance_reached rule already failed to confirm). If this rule does NOT hold for a member, that member falls to fallback:inconclusive -- an honest non-confirmation, not a forced relabel.

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a wide-token presence/equality gate (curl 'HTTP/' protocol frame; libxml2 'xml:id' attribute literal) where the winner seeds carry the exact wide token embedded in a coherent protocol/markup frame, but the assembly is too small to register a structural-tag count and too dispersed for byte-frequency or single-operand Hamming distance -- all three built tools have now been tried and none discriminates
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: These three are the value_distance_reached members that came back rule_not_met, and unlike the harfbuzz pair they have NO valid-tag vocabulary for joint_necessity to count: curl's 'HTTP/' frame and libxml2's 'xml:id' attribute are single wide tokens inside a small text/markup frame, not a multi-entry table directory, so struct_size_and_token_count has nothing to count (vpc_tags == vp_tags == 0-or-1, no tag_lift). The other untried tools also cannot discriminate: depth_reach measures ctx/ngram ITERATION depth (these gates are one-shot presence checks at L646/L1415/L472, not deep loops -- wrong mechanism); corpus_size_ratio is a whole-corpus scalar, not seed-set-specific, and cannot isolate I2S assembly of a single token. Both prior decidable tools (operand_enrichment byte-frequency; value_distance_reached Hamming-distance) were already scored and did not hold -- a third single-token tool would be a relaxed-threshold re-test of the same observable, which is forbidden. No remaining built measurement separates 'I2S spliced the wide token into a coherent frame' from 'a sufficiently valid request/document happened to carry it' -- honest inconclusive is the correct outcome (G3). NOTE: libxml2_6233 + libxml2_7249 are sB-owned targets; this decidable:false verdict is on MECHANISM (non-discriminability of single-token assembly), NOT corpus-absence -- the owning server reaches the same conclusion, so it is a valid cross-server inconclusive, not a running-server artifact.

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a presence/ID-registration gate that fires only after a CHAIN of DTD/declaration tokens is assembled (no single comparand operand) -- mechanism plausibly I2S multi-token assembly, but no built tool discriminates it after byte-frequency, single-operand Hamming distance, and token-count have all failed
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: These gates are presence_or_nonnull / dispersed_multibyte with operand_literal == null: they fire on an ASSEMBLED chain (ATTLIST + ID-type declaration + matching attribute instance for 6936/6929; DTD + xmlns-default ATTLIST for 6740), not on landing one comparand. (1) value_distance_reached needs a single concrete multi-byte operand to form a Hamming window; there is no single operand here -- the chain spans variable-position DTD keywords -> already accounted as non-applicable, and the operand-bearing siblings (6233/7249) that DID have a literal still came back rule_not_met, so the no-literal chain is a fortiori non-discriminable. (2) joint_necessity counts a structural-TAG vocabulary; XML DTD chains have no fixed table-tag vocabulary the tool counts, and a single valid document need not carry MORE distinct keyword tokens than a blocking seed, so token-count does not lift (this is the assembly-count observable, and it is the family round-1 already tried and refuted). (3) depth_reach is ctx/ngram iteration depth (these are one-shot presence checks, not deep loops). (4) corpus_size_ratio is whole-corpus, not seed-set-specific. operand_enrichment already washed out (dispersed, null literal). No built measurement separates 'I2S assembled the chain' from 'a valid-enough document happened to register an ID' -- honest inconclusive (G3), not a relabel of the same refuted assembly story.

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a single-byte equality at a content-dependent cursor or an escape-trigger byte -- I2S substitution is the plausible story but no built tool can separate it from baseline byte frequency in a text/markup corpus
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: libxml2_6643 ('?'=0x3f after a variable-length element Name) and libxml2_6809 (control byte 0x0A/0x0D/0x09 inside CDATA) are SINGLE-byte equalities at a CONTENT-DEPENDENT cursor -- no fixed gate offset for operand_enrichment, and width-1 operands are flagged chance-alignment-prone by the registry AND occur abundantly in any XML/text corpus regardless of fuzzer, so an enrichment or distance signal cannot separate I2S substitution from baseline byte presence. value_distance_reached needs width>=3 to form a meaningful Hamming gradient (a 1-byte equality has a degenerate distance of 0 or 1, below the registry's width<=2 caveat). joint_necessity / depth_reach / corpus_size_ratio are all the wrong observable for a single content-dependent byte. No built tool discriminates -- honest inconclusive (G3). sB-owned targets: verdict is on MECHANISM (single-byte non-discriminability), not corpus-absence.

hypotheses.label: vpc resolves a single-byte escape gate (curl formtable escape constant) or a derived-integer ICC header assembly (lcms tag-table type-base read) -- both currently UNSCORABLE for seed-starvation (W<3), routed to rank-10 re-bisect; mechanism untestable until more resolving seeds are recovered
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: These two are seed_starved, NOT mechanism-refuted: curl_263 has W=1 L=15 and lcms_2334 has W=1 L=10, both below the >=3 W/>=3 L floor every seed-dependent built tool requires (operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, joint_necessity, depth_reach all need >=3 each). They are flagged as rank-10 RE-BISECT candidates -- the owning server should re-run seed_bisect.py to recover additional resolving seeds, after which they can be re-scored. Independent of seed-starvation, their mechanisms are also low-discriminability: curl_263 is a single-byte escape constant (0x22/0x0D/0x0A at a content-dependent *src cursor; width-1 chance-alignment-prone, abundant in any form corpus) and lcms_2334 is a derived_integer/dispersed_multibyte header assembly (operand_literal == null; the resolve depends on a profile-signature + tag-directory-offset combination producing a successful type-base read, not a single landable operand) -- and lcms has no on-disk corpus on either server anyway (data_reality). So even post-re-bisect these are likely honest inconclusive; for now they are decidable:false pending re-bisect (G3).

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor used (I2S is untagged in mutation_op) -- confirmation is via corpus byte-frequency (literal gate), seed-set Hamming-distance-to-operand (multi-byte gradient-stall gate), or seed-set structural size+tag count (font assembly-depth gate), the only observables of I2S substitution / assembly.

value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator: BOTH the i2s_multibyte_operand_gradient_stall and the NEW i2s_font_structural_assembly_depth tests exploit exactly this -- vp carries the SAME Hamming feedback as vpc, so the vpc-vs-vp distance gap (resp. tag/size gap) isolates the I2SRandReplace MUTATOR, never a lineage gradient event. The effect is read off what the corpus REACHES / ASSEMBLES, per data_realities.

Structural-assembly gates have DIFFERENT-sized winning vs blocking seeds, so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio) is NOT used for the new assembly hypothesis -- joint_necessity uses median size + alignment-robust structural-tag COUNT, exactly as data_realities prescribe for different-sized structural gates.

on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4 -> the NEW i2s_font_structural_assembly_depth test scores harfbuzz_5745 + harfbuzz_5362 on s4 (no cross-server dependency, both harfbuzz). The frozen value_distance_reached members libxml2_6233/7249 remain sB-owned (arbitrated by their server, frozen rule).

seed-dependent tools need resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds (>=3 each) in db/blockers.sqlite. curl_263 (W=1) and lcms_2334 (W=1) are below floor -> decidable:false + rank-10 re-bisect (NOT a mechanism refutation); the harfbuzz assembly-depth members are seed-present per the round-2 assignments.
notes: ROUND-3 (FINAL) re-design of i2s_vp__LWL (78 confirmed FROZEN + 12 unlabeled). PRIORS KEPT (superset/monotonic, verbatim): (1) i2s_exact_literal_gate (operand_enrichment) -- frozen:true, the 78 confirmed branches re-validate. (2) i2s_multibyte_operand_gradient_stall (value_distance_reached) -- frozen:true, KEEP per the brief (confirmed >=1 branch); its 5 listed members came back rule_not_met for THEM (wide/dispersed FOURCC + byte-soup losers let a sliding Hamming window find coincidental near-matches, so distance_gap washed out -- the registry's dispersed-operand caveat), so those members are RE-ROUTED below without disturbing the frozen rule. FORBIDDEN re-tests honored: round-1's i2s_structural_token_assembly (joint_necessity token_lift on XML chains) and i2s_cursor_relative_single_byte, and a relaxed re-run of operand_enrichment/value_distance_reached, are NOT re-proposed for the branches they already failed on. NEW DECIDABLE MECHANISM: i2s_font_structural_assembly_depth (2 branches: harfbuzz_5745, harfbuzz_5362) -- uses the UNTRIED joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) tool, registry anchor harfbuzz/6814, validated on the harfbuzz sfnt-tag vocabulary. The novel observable is the COUNT of valid sfnt table tags + median seed SIZE in the vpc-resolving vs value_profile-blocking seed sets: the card evidence shows these winners are 'a consistent structured table directory' / 'a valid sfnt font' while losers are 'unstructured byte soup' / 'mangled/short blobs', so the resolve is structural ASSEMBLY DEPTH, not single-operand placement (which the prior value_distance_reached rule already failed to confirm). This is genuinely different (G1) from both refuted decidable tests (byte-frequency at one offset; Hamming distance to one operand) and is discriminating (if vp's gradient could build the directory, vp_tags ~ vpc_tags and the claim refutes; if the resolve were single-operand, tag-count would not lift). G2 independence: vp and vpc share the CMP_MAP feedback, so the tag/size gap is attributable to the I2S mutator. DECIDABLE:FALSE (G3) -- 8 branches across three honest-inconclusive sub-groups: (A) i2s_wide_token_assembly_undecidable = curl_427 + libxml2_6233 + libxml2_7249 (the value_distance_reached members with NO tag vocabulary for joint_necessity to count -- single wide token in a small frame; all three built single-token observables tried, none discriminates); (B) i2s_structural_chain_assembly_undecidable = libxml2_6740/6929/6936 (operand_literal-null DTD/ID CHAIN gates -- token-count already refuted family-wide, no remaining tool separates I2S assembly from a merely valid document); (C) i2s_single_byte_cursor_undecidable = libxml2_6643/6809 (single content-dependent byte -- width-1 chance-alignment-prone, abundant in any text corpus). SEED-STARVED / RE-BISECT: i2s_seed_starved_rebisect = curl_263 + lcms_2334 (W=1 each, below the >=3 floor) -> decidable:false + rebisect_candidate:true (route to rank-10 re-bisect; NOT a mechanism refutation). SUMMARY: 2 priors KEPT (78 frozen + the frozen value_distance_reached rule), 1 NEW decidable mechanism (2 branches), 3 decidable:false sub-groups (3 + 3 + 2 = 8 branches), 2 seed-starved re-bisect (2 branches). 12 unlabeled fully accounted for (2 newly testable + 8 honest inconclusive + 2 re-bisect); 90 total with the frozen 78. This is the FINAL round: the unlabeled set is dry of further built-tool-discriminable mechanisms beyond the harfbuzz assembly-depth pair.
